title: vigna unguiculata subsp. burundiensis
intro: Vigna unguiculata subsp. burundiensis, also known as the Burundi cowpea or Burundi bean, is a subspecies of the cowpea plant (Vigna unguiculata) native to Africa. This plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Burundi, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory effects. The Burundi cowpea has been shown to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and aiding in the management of diabetes and hypertension. In herbal preparations, the leaves, seeds, and roots of the Burundi cowpea are used to make infusions, decoctions, and powders, which are often consumed as teas or added to food to harness its medicinal properties.
